reasoning: GEOMETRY CHECK: (1) Macro-feature count: ~10-12 distinct cobble stone shapes visible per 32x32 tile — at the upper boundary of the 3-8 target range, verging on busy. (2) Largest feature scale: individual cobble stones estimated ~4-6px each in native tile space, which is appropriate. (3) vs 32x32 player sprite: the tile reads quieter than a character sprite in isolation, but the scattered pink/reddish accent dots raise visual energy and introduce a second layer of noise that competes slightly. The beige-tan cobble palette is reasonable for a path tile and the stones are individually readable, but the pink scatter elements feel like a separate texture layer that adds confusion. When tiled, the pink accents would repeat in a pattern that may distract from sprites sitting on top. Density is marginally acceptable but not clean enough for confident pass.
